name: SSH_Protocol.Transport.Kex

documentation.description:
Return the crypto operations bundle for this KEX algorithm instance.
Transport sets this on the session after algorithm negotiation.

subprograms.name: Compute_H
Compute_H_GEX

documentation.description:
RFC 4253, Section 7.2: Key Derivation
Uses directional key lengths so each direction can have different
cipher/MAC algorithms (RFC 4253 allows independent per-direction choice).

subprograms.name: Derive_Keys

formals.name: Compute_Shared_Secret

documentation.description:
Select a DH group (p, g) given client size criteria (min, n, max).
Used only for Group_Exchange flow; ignored for Standard_DH.

formals.name: Generate_Keypair

documentation.description:
Select a DH group (p, g) given client size criteria (min, n, max).
Used only for Group_Exchange flow; ignored for Standard_DH.

Select a DH group (p, g) given client size criteria (min, n, max).
Used only for Group_Exchange flow; ignored for Standard_DH.

Select a DH group (p, g) given client size criteria (min, n, max).
Used only for Group_Exchange flow; ignored for Standard_DH.

Fixed public key length in bytes.
0 = variable-length mpint (traditional DH) -> use trailing-zero trimming.
> 0 = fixed-length byte string (e.g. 32 for Curve25519 per RFC 8731).

PQ/T hybrid KEX (draft-ietf-sshm-mlkem-hybrid-kex-10):
K = HASH(K_PQ || K_CL) is a hash output, not an integer.
When True, Compute_H and Derive_Keys encode K as SSH string instead of mpint.
